body.u: I left school at the age of sixteen years and 1 month. The first thing I can remember is my parents saying, you’d better go and find a job, you can’t live in this day and age. IfactIn fact I ended up going on the dole. The dole was quite good but you didntdidn't get enough, especialyespecially as you had to pay mother some.
I was on the dole for 6 months, I eventually got a job in “Abbots” The money was good, about £37.00 a week.
On my 18th Birthday I moved out of home and started living with a boyfriend on which I was getting quite serious about. We eventually got married. I was 21 years of age and he, 26 years of age.

We had our first child when I was 23, it was a little girl. We called her Sarah. When I had her, I had to leave work and start hunting for a house. We eventually got a perfect house in the country for £20,000.
Our second child arrived 3 years later when I was 26. It was a boy called John. We had no more children just the two.

My mother and father died in a car crash a few years later. I missed them. I made the rest of my life misserablemiserable. Things started going wrong with the marragemarriage. And eventually, not on my side mind, we got a devorsedivorce I was nearly 40. The children went into their father’s passestionpossession. I saw then every weekend.
Then when I was 45 I met a man aged 44. We fell in love and got married. We had no children.

My ex-husband died at the age of 52 and I was quite upset about it all. We went and moved into scotlandScotland where my husband died, through a heart failiarfailure. My son and daughter were now old enough to go their different ways, Sarah was 30 and John 27. Sarah was married and John ingagedengaged. I moved to Kent where I then retired.
